originalAlarm.description: Dereference of null pointer (loaded from variable '__ops_1')

sanitizedDescription: Dereference of null pointer (loaded from variable 'ops')
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN () Bool
  true)
reasoning: 6252547b8a7.c L12: Null dereference at `int irq = *ops` where ops=NULL (set at L19, only reassigned to non-null when CONFIG_OF_IRQ=T at L22). Known bug (// ERROR at L12). Real condition: CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE=T AND CONFIG_OF_IRQ=F (ops stays NULL). The derived macro: TWL4030=T → CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN defined (L3-5). PC: (!TWL4030 AND IRQ_DOMAIN) OR TWL4030 = IRQ_DOMAIN=T (since TWL4030→IRQ_DOMAIN). The PC includes TWL4030=F AND IRQ_DOMAIN=T — in those configs irq_domain_add is defined but twl_probe (the only caller that passes NULL ops) is NOT compiled (inside #ifdef TWL4030). The null deref is unreachable with TWL4030=F. PC under-constrained — should be TWL4030=T AND OF_IRQ=F, but includes TWL4030=F cases where the bug cannot occur.
